user_query:
I’m drafting an op-ed for my newsletter arguing that the digitization of music diminishes the value of physical albums. Please use the local files to ground the piece in data and my own notes, and produce both intermediate data artifacts and the final article.

What I need you to do:
1) Run the provided Python script to generate library metrics:
   - Command to run: python3 tools/summarize_album_data.py
   - This must create outputs/data/metrics.json. Do not hand-calculate these values; use the script.
2) Extract structured data from the local HTML snapshot:
   - Parse the table with id="used-prices" in input/discogs_snapshot.html and save it as a CSV at outputs/data/used_prices.csv with headers: title,median_price_usd (numeric, no currency symbol).
3) Write the op-ed at outputs/article/physical-albums-op-ed.md that is 600–900 words and clearly takes the stance that digitization diminishes the value of physical albums. Requirements for the article:
   - Use at least two verbatim quotes from input/notes.md (put them in quotation marks and attribute to “personal notes”).
   - Include a section titled “Data points” containing a concise bullet list that explicitly includes all of the following, pulled from outputs/data/metrics.json and outputs/data/used_prices.csv:
     • Physical share in my library (as a percentage from metrics.json, labeled “Physical share”).
     • Total album count (from metrics.json, labeled “Total albums”).
     • Estimated replacement cost of my library (USD from metrics.json, labeled “Estimated replacement cost”).
     • Median used price for “Kind of Blue” (USD from used_prices.csv).
     • Median used price for “Random Access Memories” (USD from used_prices.csv).
   - Include a section titled “Methodology & sources” that briefly explains that the figures came from input/library.json and input/pressing_costs.csv via tools/summarize_album_data.py, and from parsing input/discogs_snapshot.html.
   - Keep the tone reflective but evidence-backed; avoid external sources—only use data derived from the provided files.

Deliverables to produce:
- outputs/data/metrics.json (via the provided script, not manual editing)
- outputs/data/used_prices.csv (parsed from the HTML file)
- outputs/article/physical-albums-op-ed.md (the final op-ed)

Use only the provided files and make sure the numbers in the article match the two generated data files exactly. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
